The \\\\u00b7 amendments in this Update include items raised for Board consideration through \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nAccounting Standards Update No. 2016-09, Compensation\\\\u2014Stock Compensation \\\\u00b7 (Topic 718): Improvements to Employee Share-Based Payment Accounting, is \\\\u00b7 unclear on whether an entity should recognize excess tax benefits (or tax \\\\u00b7 deficiencies) for compensation expense that is taken on the entity\\\\u2019s tax return. The \\\\u00b7 amendment to paragraph 718-740-35-2 in this Update clarifies that an entity \\\\u00b7 should recognize excess tax benefits (that is, the difference in tax benefits between \\\\u00b7 the deduction for tax purposes and the compensation cost recognized for financial \\\\u00b7 statement reporting) in the period in which the amount of the deduction is \\\\u00b7 determined. In \\\\u00b7 other words, each of the goods or services is significantly affected by one \\\\u00b7 or more of the other goods or services in the contract. For example, in \\\\u00b7 some cases, two or more goods or services are significantly affected by \\\\u00b7 each other because the entity would not be able to fulfill its promise by \\\\u00b7 transferring each of the goods or services independently.The good or \\\\u00b7 service is not highly dependent on, or highly interrelated with, other goods ... Patents, trademarks, and copyrights. 606-10-55-55 In addition to a promise to grant a license (or licenses) to a ... Functional intellectual property. General) and some jurisdictions follow trust law. 958-205-05-9 Because donor stipulations and laws vary, NFPs must assess the \\\\u00b7\\\",\\n2350:    \\\"text\\\": \\\"OK here's the full picture. It's not a mafia situation \\\\u2014 it's actually the opposite. The door IS open, it's just that the history makes it *look* like there's a secret handshake.\\\\n\\\\n**The timeline that creates the confusion:**\\\\n\\\\nBefore February 2023, the FASB Codification had two tiers: a crippled \\\\\\\"Basic View\\\\\\\" (free, but no search, no copy/paste, limited navigation) and a \\\\\\\"Professional View\\\\\\\" ($940/year per user). PwC, Deloitte, KPMG, Thomson Reuters Checkpoint, Wolters Kluwer CCH \\\\u2014 they all had **licensing agreements** with the Financial Accounting Foundation to reproduce the full codification text within their paid platforms. That's the commercial arrangement: FAF licenses the text, firms pay to embed it in their research tools alongside their own interpretive guides.\\\\n\\\\n**Then the SEC's Investor Advisory Committee told FAF to stop gatekeeping.** In January 2023, FAF announced it was eliminating the paid tier entirely, and in February 2023 they launched full free access at **asc.fasb.org**. The Professional View is gone. Everyone gets everything \\\\u2014 full search, copy/paste, printing, the whole codification, every paragraph of every topic.\\\\n\\\\n**What PwC Viewpoint actually is:**\\\\n\\\\nTwo layers, and the trick is understanding which is which:\\\\n\\\\n1. **Free tier (no account needed):** All PwC guides (the 30+ accounting and reporting guides we identified before), all news, all \\\\\\\"In depth\\\\\\\" publications, AND \\\\u2014 here's the key \\\\u2014 **all US GAAP and US GASB content is free.** That includes the FASB Codification embedded in their platform. Their own pricing flyer says it explicitly: *\\\\\\\"US GAAP and US GASB related content is available free of charge by registering for a free Viewpoint account.\\\\\\\"*\\\\n\\\\n2. **Paid tier ($$$):** PwC's proprietary Accounting and Reporting Manual (ARM), the SEC Volume, IFRS content (which requires a separate license from the IFRS Foundation), and international content. This is PwC's *own* work product \\\\u2014 their internal guidance manuals that their audit teams use.\\\\n\\\\n**The actual access paths you have RIGHT NOW:**\\\\n\\\\n**Path 1: asc.fasb.org (the source)**\\\\n- Full FASB Codification, all ~90 topics, every paragraph\\\\n- Free registration, full search, copy/paste enabled\\\\n- No API, no bulk download \\\\u2014 it's a web application\\\\n- But you CAN manually navigate topic by topic and copy text\\\\n- The terms say \\\\\\\"personal non-commercial use\\\\\\\" unless you get written permission \\\\u2014 but this is the same copyright gray area we discussed (private body writing what functions as law)\\\\n\\\\n**Path 2: Every single ASU (Accounting Standards Update) is a free PDF on fasb.org**\\\\n- These are the *amendments* to the Codification\\\\n- They contain the **full verbatim text** of every paragraph being added/changed/deleted\\\\n- They're at storage.fasb.org \\\\u2014 direct PDF links\\\\n- Example: ASU 2014-09 (Revenue Recognition/ASC 606) contains the entire Topic 606 text\\\\n- You can reconstruct major portions of the current Codification by collecting all the ASUs\\\\n\\\\n**Path 3: PwC Viewpoint free tier**\\\\n- Register free at viewpoint.pwc.com\\\\n- Full ASU text is hosted on their platform (I found verbatim ASC 606 paragraphs there)\\\\n- All their accounting guides contain labeled ASC excerpts with paragraph citations\\\\n- Their guides state this explicitly: *\\\\\\\"excerpts from the FASB's Accounting Standards Codification... clearly labelled\\\\\\\"*\\\\n\\\\n**Path 4: Deloitte DART**\\\\n- The paid tier includes a full Codification browser (their FASB ASC Codification Manual)\\\\n- But the free Roadmap guides contain extensive ASC quotes\\\\n- Every ASU analysis on dart.deloitte.com includes the amendment text\\\\n\\\\n**So what's the actual play for building a GAAP knowledge base?**\\\\n\\\\nYou don't need to scrape anything or find a secret door. The strategy is layered:\\\\n\\\\n1. **Primary source:** Go to asc.fasb.org, register free, and systematically read/copy the topics relevant to your knowledge base. For a tax advisory practice, you probably need maybe 15-20 topics max (revenue recognition, income taxes, leases, financial instruments, etc.)\\\\n\\\\n2. **Supplementary:** Download the key ASU PDFs from storage.fasb.org \\\\u2014 these are unrestricted PDFs on a public server. They contain full paragraph text with tracked changes showing exactly what was added/removed.\\\\n\\\\n3. **Interpretation layer:** PwC's free guides on Viewpoint give you the Big 4's read on how each standard actually works in practice, with direct ASC citations. These are arguably *more* useful than the raw codification because they include the Q&As and examples that practitioners actually need.\\\\n\\\\n4. **The XBRL Taxonomy** (the one I found earlier with the open license) \\\\u2014 this gives you the complete structural map of every element in the Codification. It's machine-readable, explicitly allows reproduction in \\\\\\\"Permitted Works that comment on, explain, or assist in the use or implementation\\\\\\\" \\\\u2014 and includes every paragraph reference.\\\\n\\\\nThe \\\\\\\"mafia\\\\\\\" perception comes from the *old* regime (pre-2023) when FAF was charging $940/year and licensing to the Big 4. The SEC essentially forced them to open it up. The Big 4 still have licensing agreements for embedding the text in their commercial platforms, but you don't need their platforms to get the text \\\\u2014 you can get it from the source for free.\\\",\\n\",\"stderr\":\"\"}",
        "uuid": "83fe3987-2be8-4022-81ff-e7cd4e5abbe4"
      }
    ],
    "is_error": false,

    "display_content": {
      "type": "json_block",
      "json_block": "{\"returncode\":0,\"stdout\":\"220:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"By having consistently applied \\\\u201caccounting rules\\\\u201d, results and data can be compared across entities by users of the financial statements. The FAF states that \\\\u201chigh quality financial accounting and reporting standards promote better information in the marketplace. Better information fosters greater transparency. Transparent, relevant information helps investors and lenders make better decisions about where to put their money with confidence.\\\\u201d \\\\u00b7 The FASB standards are easy to find (and they are free)! Simply go to the FASB\\\\u2019s website, select \\\\u201cStandards\\\\u201d at the top of the site, and then \\\\u201cAccounting Standards Codification\\\\u201d. Or, you can select the direct link to the ASC near the top of the FASB\\\\u2019s homepage. Once you\\\\u2019ve found your way to the ASC, you must check that you are not a robot and then select \\\\u201cAccess\\\\u201d. Once you\\\\u2019ve entered the ASC (and accepted a licensing agreement), the guidance is ready for your research!\\\\n\\\\nBy having consistently applied \\\\u201caccounting rules\\\\u201d, results and data can be compared across entities by users of the financial statements. The FAF states that \\\\u201chigh quality financial accounting and reporting standards promote better information in the marketplace. Better information fosters greater transparency. Transparent, relevant information helps investors and lenders make better decisions about where to put their money with confidence.\\\\u201d \\\\u00b7 The FASB standards are easy to find (and they are free)! Simply go to the FASB\\\\u2019s website, select \\\\u201cStandards\\\\u201d at the top of the site, and then \\\\u201cAccounting Standards Codification\\\\u201d. Or, you can select the direct link to the ASC near the top of the FASB\\\\u2019s homepage. Once you\\\\u2019ve found your way to the ASC, you must check that you are not a robot and then select \\\\u201cAccess\\\\u201d. Once you\\\\u2019ve entered the ASC (and accepted a licensing agreement), the guidance is ready for your research!\\\\n\\\\nThis blog will discuss the \\\\u201caccounting rules\\\\u201d found in the FASB Accounting Standards Codification (ASC), such as who establishes the content, how to locate and navigate, and how new rules are created. The Financial Accounting Foundation (FAF) has two standard-setting boards: the Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) and the Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB). These boards work together to develop U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (U.S. GAAP). State and local governments use GASB established standards, while public and private entities and non-profits use FASB established standards (we\\\\u2019ll focus on the FASB standards in this post). The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has designated the FASB to be the standard setter for public companies and many other organizations have also recognized the FASB\\\\u2019s standards to be authoritative as well.\\\\n\\\\nSome industries have unique transactions that require specific guidance, so the FASB created industry focused topics within the ASC. It\\\\u2019s important to note that the industry specific guidance is incremental, entities must also follow guidance in other Topics that do not conflict with the industry-specific guidance. There are currently 15 industry topics in the ASC, such as Agriculture, Airlines, Entertainment, Extractive Activities, Financial Services, Software, and U.S. Steamship Entities. We frequently get asked, \\\\u201cHow is accounting guidance issued by the SEC incorporated into the ASC?\\\\u201d \\\\u00b7 Since the FASB is not responsible for issuing or maintaining SEC guidance, the SEC guidance is not the same as the other guidance found in the ASC. However, as a convenience to its users, the FASB acknowledged that this information is essential for the financial reporting of public companies and, as a result, has incorporated certain guidance from the SEC into the ASC.\\\\n\\\\nThe accounting world is constantly changing, which means the \\\\u201caccounting rules\\\\u201d must change too! Typically, the FASB (Board) identifies a topic or issue via requests from users or other means. Research is conducted and conversations occur amongst the Board at public meetings regarding the issues identified and analyzed by the FASB staff. Exposure drafts are then issued to obtain public input, and, in some cases, public meetings are conducted. The responses from the public are submitted through \\\\u201cComment Letters.\\\\u201d The comment letters and public discussions are reviewed at a subsequent public meeting by the Board before an Accounting Standard Update (ASU) is drafted, voted upon by the Board, and issued. The ASUs issued describe what is changing and the logic behind the changes as well as the effective date of the changes. ASUs are numbered based on the year issued and the update number in a sequential order (e.g., ASU 2021-01 would signify the first ASU issued in the year 2021).\\\",\\n371:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"It includes citations to the FASB&#x27;s Accounting Standards Codification organized alphabetically by topic. There is a section of &quot;uncertain tax positions&quot; which is one of the ACC 550 research topics. The library also owns hard copies of the Accounting Policy &amp; Practice Portfolios. They can be found in the Reference section (REF HF 5616 .U5 .A345). Each portfolio will bear a number after the call number. For example: Accounting for Income Taxes: Uncertain Tax Positions (FIN 48) (REF HF 5616.45 .A345 No. 5002) Accounting for Income Taxes: Uncertain Tax Positions - Selected Topics (REF HF 5616.45 .A345 No. 5003) More information: View a list of all portfolios. Checkpoint is the best database to use to find Codification standards. ... This library handout has search tips on how to use the database. ... You can use the RIA Checkpoint database to access the FASB pre-codification standards.\\\\n\\\\nRIA Checkpoint database will provide you with access to Accounting and Auditing materials, including the new FASB Accounting Standards Codification and the prior standards and codes. ... Go to the index of these reference books and look up your topic for more information. Georgiades, G. (2011). GAAP Financial statement disclosures manual. Chicago: CCH. (REF HF 5658 .M55 2018-19) This reference book is a source of financial statement disclosures and key presentation requirements. There are hundreds of examples of sample footnote disclosures. Use this reference work to look up an ASC topic and get an executive summary, the disclosure and key presentation requirements, and examples of financial statement disclosures. Topics are arranged numerically into 9 parts, consistent with the organization of the Codification. Part 1: General Principles (ASC Topics 100s) Part 2: Presentation (ASC Topics 200s) Part 3: Assets (ASC Topics 300s) Part 4: Liabilities (ASC Topics 400s) Part 5: Equity (ASC T\\\\n\\\\n(REF HF 5681 .B2 .A35) The annual reference work compiles annual reporting and disclosure data with examples drawn from a survey of the annual reports of some 500 publicly traded companies. ASC (Accounting Standards Codification) is included when referenced in the statements of the reporting entities. Siegel, J. G., Levine, M. H., Qureshi, A. A., & Shim, J. K. (2018). GAAP Handbook of policies and procedures. Chicago: CCH. (REF HF 5616 .U5 .S54) This practical reference book contains all of the important authoritative pronouncements on GAAP. It contains accounting principles, financial reporting presentation requirements, recommended disclosures, and specialized accounting topics. Williams, J. R., Carcello, J. V., & Neal, T. L. (2011). GAAP Guide. Chicago: CCH. (REF HF 5616 .U5 .M526 2011) 2 vols. This reference guide analyzes AICPA Accounting Research Bulletins, APB Opinions, and FASB Statements and Interpretations.\\\\n\\\\nIt includes citations to the FASB's Accounting Standards Codification organized alphabetically by topic. There is a section of \\\\\\\"uncertain tax positions\\\\\\\" which is one of the ACC 550 research topics. The library also owns hard copies of the Accounting Policy & Practice Portfolios. They can be found in the Reference section (REF HF 5616 .U5 .A345). Each portfolio will bear a number after the call number. For example: Accounting for Income Taxes: Uncertain Tax Positions (FIN 48) (REF HF 5616.45 .A345 No. 5002) Accounting for Income Taxes: Uncertain Tax Positions - Selected Topics (REF HF 5616.45 .A345 No. 5003) More information: View a list of all portfolios. Checkpoint is the best database to use to find Codification standards. ... This library handout has search tips on how to use the database. ... You can use the RIA Checkpoint database to access the FASB pre-codification standards.\\\\n\\\\nOn July 1, 2009 the Financial Accounting standards Board (FASB) instituted a major change in the way accounting standards are organized. The FASB Accounting Standards Codification (FASB Codification) became the single official source of authoritative, nongovernmental U.S. generally accepted accounting principles (U.S. GAAP). Now only one level of authoritative U.S. GAAP exists, other than guidance issued by the SEC. All other literature is non-authoritative. The Codification reorganizes thousands of U.S. GAAP pronouncements into roughly 90 accounting Topics, and displays all Topics using a consistent structure. Also included is relevant SEC guidance that follows the same topical structure used in the Codification. The reorganization is thought to simplify U.S. GAAP authoritative literature. The Codification includes the following authoritative literature issued by standard setters other than the SEC that apply to all entities (other than governmental entities): \\\\u2022 Financial Accounting S\\\",\\n389:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Topic 105, Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, for additional details. The Codification structure is significantly different from the structure of previous standards. This document provides information that will help in obtaining a good understanding of the \\\\u00b7 Codification structure, ...\\\\n\\\\nTopic 105, Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, for additional details. The Codification structure is significantly different from the structure of previous standards. This document provides information that will help in obtaining a good understanding of the \\\\u00b7 Codification structure, content, and style. ... System) streamlines the research process. The Codification Research System has numerous ... Derivative Instruments and Hedging Activities, is an amendment of FASB Statement No. 133, Accounting for Derivative Instruments and Hedging Activities, so the content of Statement 149 is \\\\u00b7 included through the as-amended version of Statement 133. As of July 1, 2009, the Codification was composed of the following literature issued by various ... Commission approval. If you find any discrepancies between the SEC content as presented in the Codification and the \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nThe FASB developed a hybrid classification system specifically for the Codification. The following is the structure of the classification system: XXX-YY-ZZ-PP where XXX = Topic, YY = Subtopic, ZZ = Section, PP = Paragraph In \\\\u00b7 the case of SEC content, an \\\\u201cS\\\\u201d precedes the Section number. ... Topics represent a collection of related guidance. For example, Leases is a Topic. The Topics \\\\u00b7 correlate very closely to standards issued by the International Accounting Standards Board ... Topic or by general Topic. ... Subtopics represent subsets of a Topic and are generally distinguished by type or by scope. For \\\\u00b7 example, Operating Leases and Capital Leases are two Subtopics of the Leases Topic \\\\u00b7 distinguished by type of lease. Each Topic contains an Overall Subtopic that generally represents \\\\u00b7 the pervasive guidance for the Topic. Each additional Subtopic represents incremental or unique \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nTopic. The use of a consistent code allows the FASB to create one version of the content that \\\\u00b7 resides in a single location but allows users to access the same content from multiple locations in \\\\u00b7 the navigation structure. For example, a user can access the system from the perspective of the \\\\u00b7 agriculture industry looking for inventory content. Alternatively, the user could access inventory \\\\u00b7 and find the agriculture industry content. In this case, Codification Topic 330 represents \\\\u00b7 Inventory and Codification Topic 905 represents the Agriculture Industry. As noted above, industry content resides in the Industry Topic. Therefore, Codification Subtopic \\\\u00b7 905-330 represents the Inventory Subtopic of the Agriculture Industry. When browsing \\\\u00b7 Codification Topic 330, users will see Subtopic 905\\\\u2014Agriculture. That represents an \\\\u00b7 intersection link to the content in Codification Subtopic 905-330.\\\\n\\\\nCodification Topic 105 includes a discussion of materiality. Over the years, various standards \\\\u00b7 have included language stating that a standard is applicable if an item is material. Retaining \\\\u00b7 such individual materiality references in the Codification could lead users to believe that \\\\u00b7 materiality applies to one set of standards, but not another. This is not the case. ... With the issuance of FASB Statement No. 168, The FASB Accounting Standards Codification\\\\u00ae \\\\u00b7 and the Hierarchy of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, the FASB approved the \\\\u00b7 Codification as the source of authoritative US GAAP for nongovernmental entities for interim \\\\u00b7 and annual periods ending after September 15, 2009. The Codification supersedes all existing \\\\u00b7 non-SEC standards. The Codification Research System will include the superseded standards for ... Standards Update Instructions.\\\",\\n407:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"... This Topic contains three subtopics (Overall, Discontinued Operations, and Liquidation Basis of Accounting). ... This Topic contains two Subtopics (Overall and Offsetting). ... This Topic has been moved to Topic 505, Equity, as part of the FASB\\\\u2019s project on Codification improvements.\\\\n\\\\nASC 995 U.S. Steamship Entities ... The Codification\\\\u2019s categories are organized by Topic, Subtopic, Section, and paragraph, each with a numerical designation. The Sections in every Subtopic are labeled uniformly, as follows: The Codification is updated via Accounting Standards Updates (ASUs). These are assigned a number that corresponds to the year of the ASU\\\\u2019s issuance and its sequential order (e.g., the first ASU issued in 2010 was 2010-01). ASUs replace accounting changes that historically were issued as FASB Statements, FASB Interpretations, FASB Staff Positions, or other types of FASB standards. ASUs contain a background and basis for conclusions as well as a marked draft of any changes to existing guidance. The new guidance is labeled \\\\u201cPending Content\\\\u201d in the Codification, and the superseded guidance will not be removed until the guidance in the ASU is effective for all entities.\\\\n\\\\nThe FASB Accounting Standards Codification is the source of authoritative generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) recognized by the FASB to be applied to nongovernmental entities. The Codification is effective for interim and annual periods ending after September 15, 2009. ... You must log in to view this content and have a subscription package that includes this content. ... This Topic establishes the FASB Accounting Standards Codification (Codification) as the source of authoritative GAAP recognized by the FASB to be applied by nongovernmental entities. ... This Topic contains three subtopics (Overall, Discontinued Operations, and Liquidation Basis of Accounting). ... This Topic contains two Subtopics (Overall and Offsetting). ... This Topic has been moved to Topic 505, Equity, as part of the FASB\\\\u2019s project on Codification improvements.\\\\n\\\\nThis Topic has been moved to Topic 210, Balance Sheet, as part of the FASB\\\\u2019s project on Codification improvements. ... This Topic provides general guidance for receivables and notes that receivables arise from credit sales, loans, or other transactions. ... This Topic provides detailed guidance on the accounting and reporting of \\\\u201cinvestments in equity securities that have readily determinable fair values\\\\u201d and \\\\u201call investments in debt securities.\\\\u201d ... This Topic provides detailed guidance for \\\\u201cinvestments in equity securities and other ownership interests in an entity, including investments in partnerships, unincorporated joint ventures, and limited liability companies as if those other ownership interests are equity securities.\\\\u201d ... This Topic comprises three Subtopics (Overall; Partnerships, Joint Ventures, and Limited Liability Entities; and Income Taxes).\\\\n\\\\nThis Topic simply provides a link to the guidance in the Codification on deferred revenue related to vendor sales incentives, which is in ASC 605-50. ... This Topic provides guidance for general commitments, such as \\\\u201cunused letters of credit; preferred stock dividends in arrears; commitments such as those for plant acquisition; and obligations to reduce debts, maintain working capital, or restrict dividends.\\\\u201d ... The Codification supersedes all existing \\\\u00b7 non-SEC standards. The Codification Research System will include the superseded standards for ... Standards Update Instructions. The title of the combined set will be Accounting Standards \\\\u00b7 Update YYYY-XX, where YYYY is the year issued and XX is the sequential number for each \\\\u00b7 Accounting Standards Update with that year. For example, the combined numbers would be \\\\u00b7 2009-01, 2009-02, and so forth. All authoritative US GAAP issued by the FASB will be issued \\\\u00b7 in this format, regardless of the form in which such guidance may have been issued previously \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nTopic 105, Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, for additional details. The Codification structure is significantly different from the structure of previous standards. If the Cross Reference report does not list the source of the content from existing \\\\u00b7 authoritative literature, then the FASB deemed it as superseded or nonessential. The FASB developed a hybrid classification system specifically for the Codification. The following is the structure of the classification system: XXX-YY-ZZ-PP where XXX = Topic, YY = Subtopic, ZZ = Section, PP = Paragraph \\\\u00b7 In the case of SEC content, an \\\\u201cS\\\\u201d precedes the Section number. ... Topics represent a collection of related guidance. For example, Leases is a Topic. The Topics \\\\u00b7 correlate very closely to standards issued by the International Accounting Standards Board ... Topic or by general Topic. ... Subtopics represent subsets of a Topic and are generally distinguished by type or by scope. For \\\\u00b7 example, Operating Leases and Capital Leases are two Subtopics of the Leases Topic \\\\u00b7 distinguished by type of lease. An Accounting Standards Update is not authoritative; rather, it is a document that communicates how the Accounting Standards Codification is being amended. It also provides other information to help a user of GAAP understand how and why GAAP is changing and when the changes will be effective. For additional copies of this Accounting Standards Update and information on applicable prices and discount rates contact: Order Department Financial Accounting Standards Board 401 Merritt 7 PO Box 5116 Norwalk, CT 06856-5116 \\\\u00b7 Please ask for our Product Code No. ASU2020-03. FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING SERIES (ISSN 0885-9051) is published monthly with the exception of May, July, and October by the Financial Accounting Foundation, 401 Merritt 7, PO Box 5116, Norwalk, CT 06856-5116.\\\\n\\\\nThe amendments to the FASB Accounting Standards Codification \\\\u00ae in this Accounting Standards Update require improvements to the U.S. GAAP Financial Reporting Taxonomy (Taxonomy). Those improvements, which will be incorporated into the proposed 2021 Taxonomy, are available through ... Please ensure that you select Print Background (colors and images) when printing. ... To select a lower level for your search, click on one of the titles below and enter your search term to search only that level of the content. ASU 2020-03 ... Welcome to Viewpoint, the new platform that replaces Inform.\\\\n\\\\nThe FASB Accounting Standards Codification \\\\u00ae is the source of authoritative generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) recognized by the FASB to be applied by nongovernmental entities. An Accounting Standards Update is not authoritative; rather, it is a document that communicates how the Accounting Standards Codification is being amended. It also provides other information to help a user of GAAP understand how and why GAAP is changing and when the changes will be effective. For additional copies of this Accounting Standards Update and information on applicable prices and discount rates contact: Order Department Financial Accounting Standards Board 401 Merritt 7 PO Box 5116 Norwalk, CT 06856-5116 \\\\u00b7 Please ask for our Product Code No. ASU2020-03. FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING SERIES (ISSN 0885-9051) is published monthly with the exception of May, July, and October by the Financial Accounting Foundation, 401 Merritt 7, PO Box 5116, Norwalk, CT 06856-5116.\\\\n\\\\nWhy Is the FASB Issuing This Accounting Standards Update (Update)? The Board has an ongoing project on its agenda for improving the Codification or correcting its unintended application. The items addressed in that project generally are not expected to have a significant effect on current accounting practice or create a significant administrative cost for most entities. The amendments in this Update are similar to those items. However, the Board decided to issue a separate Update to improve various financial instruments Topics in the Codification to increase stakeholder awareness of the amendments and to expedite the improvement process. An explanation of why each amendment in this Update is being made is provided in the \\\\u201cAmendments to the FASB Accounting Standards Codification\\\\u00ae\\\\u201d section. Thus, there is no separate section for the Board\\\\u2019s basis for conclusions in this Update.\\\",\\n1519:    \\\"thinking\\\": \\\"Now I'm getting somewhere. Let me piece this together:\\\\n\\\\n1. Since February 2023, FAF eliminated the paid \\\\\\\"Professional View\\\\\\\" and made the FULL FASB ASC available free at asc.fasb.org\\\\n2. PwC Viewpoint has TWO tiers:\\\\n   - FREE tier: All PwC guides, news, AND all US GAAP/GASB content (including FASB Codification)\\\\n   - PAID tier: PwC's proprietary manuals (ARM - Accounting & Reporting Manual), SEC volume, and IFRS/international content\\\\n3. PwC's guides literally contain \\\\\\\"excerpts from the FASB's Accounting Standards Codification\\\\\\\" - clearly labeled, with PwC interpretation alongside\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n4. So there's no secret door here\\\\u2014the entire FASB Codification is already public and free. PwC just has a license to reproduce excerpts in their guides, same as anyone else could. The real story is that FAF actually moved away from the \\\\\\\"mafia\\\\\\\" model by making it free in 2023 after pressure from the SEC's Investor Advisory Committee, whereas they used to charge $940/year for Professional View.\\\\n\\\\nNow I should look into how other commercial databases like RIA Checkpoint fit into this picture.\\\",\\n1547:      \\\"query\\\": \\\"asc.fasb.org terms conditions copy paste scraping bulk download permitted 2024\\\"\\n2010:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Accounting Research Manager (Wolters Kluwer CCH) Materials from the AICPA, FASB, and IASB. Includes SOPs, Audit and Accounting Guides, Audit Risk Alerts, FASB Codification, International Accounting Standards (IAS), International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), and more.\\\\n\\\\nAccounting Research Manager (Wolters Kluwer CCH) Materials from the AICPA, FASB, and IASB. Includes SOPs, Audit and Accounting Guides, Audit Risk Alerts, FASB Codification, International Accounting Standards (IAS), International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), and more. AICPA Professional Library (via Thomson Reuters Checkpoint) Provides access to accounting and financial reporting analysis with a complete library of statements of position, auditing standards, audit and accounting guides, and other primary source material. ... Tax and accounting laws and news on the international, federal, state, and local levels; includes treaties, statutes, codes, rules, regulations, decisions, tax citators, IRS publications, legislative materials, current awareness tax news sources, and periodicals. International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) and International Accounting Standards (IAS) are searchable in this database.\\\\n\\\\nLibrary Guides: Accounting: Accounting Standards\\\",\\n2028:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"\\\\u201cThomson Reuters Checkpoint offers comprehensive tax and accounting research products, complete with practice aids, interactive workflow tools, diagrams and more,\\\\u201d said Adam Gretz, senior manager for the tax practitioners segment, noting that its products include a number of workflow tools, worksheets, decision trees and more. At Wolters Kluwer, Bas Kniphorst, the vice president of product management for Tax &amp; Accounting Research &amp; Learning (U.S. and Canada), said, \\\\u201cWe offer both tax research and accounting research/practice aids \\\\u2026 . CCH AnswerConnect provides tax research and the CCH Accounting Research Manager provides accounting and audit research.\\\\u201d \\\\u00b7 And Bloomberg Tax &amp; Accounting noted that it, too, has products in both areas, according to president Lisa Fitzpatrick. The Tax Book, meanwhile, is more specialized, according to its head of business development, Jacob Meyer: \\\\u201cOur primary focus is on tax research \\\\u2014 keeping accountants up to date on the ever-changing tax laws so they ca\\\\n\\\\nFinancial Accounting combines analysis and interpretations from leading experts with the latest news, FASB Codification, time-saving practice tools, and more. The new platform provides improved search and search tools such as search suggestions and BNA Picks that provide quick access to the most relevant information based on your keyword search, as well as workflow tools, including Workspace folders where you can easily print and share research across users.\\\\u201d \\\\u00b7 Finally, Wolters Kluwer\\\\u2019s Kniphorst said that the CCH AnswerConnect editorial team is constantly building out information within the platform to keep the most recent information available. As interest in topics including the TCJA grows, new Federal Topic Pages are added to provide context, examples and guidance to customers who subscribe to the platform. ... Vendors were also vocal about where the technology is taking research and practice aids. Pushkar Bhoopalam, research lead for tax product at Thomson Reuters, feels that ther\\\\n\\\\nAccording to Gretz, Thomson Reuters also had a host of products and practice aids to offer this year, including a complete analysis of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act as well as multiple TCJA-related interactive tools, including the Qualified Business Income Deduction Worksheet, the Business Interest Expense Worksheet, the Kiddie Tax Calculator, and the Choice of Entity Analyzer. \\\\u201cIn addition to the interactive tools, we also published new Checkpoint Catalyst Topics, such as \\\\u2018Qualified Business Deduction from Partnerships, S Corporations, LLCs and Sole Proprietorships (IRC \\\\u00a7 199A)\\\\u2019 and \\\\u2018Section 965 Transition Tax on Accumulated Foreign Earnings,\\\\u2019\\\\u201d he said. Bloomberg Tax & Accounting and Wolters Kluwer also introduced new products to address the changing regulation and practice scene. Fitzpatrick detailed some of Bloomberg\\\\u2019s new products: \\\\u201cAmong the most requested items from our clients are more planning tools.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\u201cThomson Reuters Checkpoint offers comprehensive tax and accounting research products, complete with practice aids, interactive workflow tools, diagrams and more,\\\\u201d said Adam Gretz, senior manager for the tax practitioners segment, noting that its products include a number of workflow tools, worksheets, decision trees and more. At Wolters Kluwer, Bas Kniphorst, the vice president of product management for Tax & Accounting Research & Learning (U.S. and Canada), said, \\\\u201cWe offer both tax research and accounting research/practice aids \\\\u2026 . CCH AnswerConnect provides tax research and the CCH Accounting Research Manager provides accounting and audit research.\\\\u201d \\\\u00b7 And Bloomberg Tax & Accounting noted that it, too, has products in both areas, according to president Lisa Fitzpatrick. The Tax Book, meanwhile, is more specialized, according to its head of business development, Jacob Meyer: \\\\u201cOur primary focus is on tax research \\\\u2014 keeping accountants up to date on the ever-changing tax laws so they ca\\\\n\\\\nAccording to Wolters Kluwer\\\\u2019s Kniphorst, \\\\u201cAs new AI technologies like machine learning, predictive analytics, and robotic process automation continue to be applied in new ways, the research experience will continue to evolve to be more intuitive and predictive, improving speed-to-answer. Wolters Kluwer also expects the integration of tax compliance workflows with research platforms to get even tighter, so professionals can spend less time researching outside of their tax compliance tools. Finally, these tools will also start offering integrated professional development opportunities with CPE integration through nano-learning with courses available directly from research topic pages and delivered through a robust online CPELink learning platform that includes on-demand webinars.\\\\u201d \\\\u00b7 And The Tax Book\\\\u2019s Meyer offered these observations: \\\\u201cEveryone is glued to their smartphones.\\\",\\n2047:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"The CCH Accounting Research Manager ... controls standards, the Financial Accounting Standards Board Codification, FASB Codification Topic 740 (FIN 48), Sarbanes-Oxley and other pertinent topics....\\\\n\\\\nIt offers an online platform from which to access appropriate related content by linking directly from documents to additional information such as the Internal Revenue Code, CCH Explanations, regulations and more; to navigate via a Browse Tree and Favorites linked to the homepage; a fast and intuitive search filter system; to print any portion of a document in one step; to view a searchable, alphabetical list of all publications subscribed to; to search selected practice areas; and to search up to eight different topics at any time. IntelliConnect not only enables the accountant to conduct searches in a myriad of ways - browse/menu walk for specific content, search selected content by keyword, search all content by keyword, search by citation -- but integrates smoothly with ProSystem fx Tax and IntelliConnect Search Express. ... Pricing: Starts at $105. Checkpoint, Thomson Reuters' online research platform, offers fast access to a variety of content from Thomson Reuters' RIA, WG&L, Wor\\\\n\\\\nPowered by IntelliConnect, an online platform, all of CCH Essentials can be searched at once and the results can be filtered, saved, printed and e-mailed. CCH Essentials through CCH Small Firm Services includes the U.S. Master Tax Guide; the Internal Revenue Code; federal tax regulations; letter rulings and Internal Revenue Service positions; IRS publications; Circular 203; and Tax Court and federal tax cases. ... Riverwoods, Ill. ... Pricing: Starts at $200. IntelliConnect, part of the Wolters Kluwer global online content delivery and publishing platform, provides accountants and other tax professionals access to the content available from CCH and other sources for more effective management of resources and research.\\\\n\\\\nThe CCH Accounting Research Manager is an online research tool that offers a comprehensive collection of resources and practice tools about tax, accounting, auditing, governmental, Securities and Exchange Commission and internal controls standards, the Financial Accounting Standards Board Codification, FASB Codification Topic 740 (FIN 48), Sarbanes-Oxley and other pertinent topics. The Accounting Research Manager database is updated up to five times per day, and includes Daily News, the latest accounting, auditing, international, regulatory and governmental news, delivered via e-mail. In addition, it helps address a variety of issues through add-on libraries, such as public and private audits, knowledge-based audits, accounting, internal controls, fraud and forensic accounting, 10-K lookup, and financial reporting in the Asia-Pacific region. To keep current, subscribers receive a Weekly Summary, an e-mail newsletter highlighting the key developments of the week that provides links to a\\\\n\\\\nCheckpoint thoroughly integrates both tax and accounting information, melding content with Thomson Reuters' compliance software, including ONESOURCE, Ultra Tax CS and e-Form RS, as well as the Thomson Reuters CPE platform, Checkpoint Learning. Checkpoint also integrates with Microsoft Office for output, and has been optimized for use with the iPad and selected smartphones. Upgrades are made directly to the platform for immediate access. Recent enhancements include the addition of The International Trade Laws of the U.S.: Statutes and Strategies; PPC's Guide to the Clarified Auditing Standards; Essential International Tax Solutions powered by Orbitax; and enhancements to the state and local tax practice area. ... Intuit Inc. Mountain View, Calif. ... http://lacerte.intuit.com/products/tax-research/ and http://proseries.intuit.com/products/tax-research/ Pricing: Unlimited annual subscription -- $849; unlimited seasonal subscription -- $449; pay-per-use subscription -- $19.95 (four articl\\\",\\n2066:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"For assistance with stories, including interviews with CCH subject experts, please contact Eric Scott 847-267-2179 eric.scott@wolterskluwer.com \\\\u00b7 The white paper was authored by the CCH Accounting Research Manager\\\\u00ae (ARM) editorial team in collaboration with Scott Taub, a former Deputy Chief Accountant at the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). During his tenure at the SEC, Taub periodically served as Acting Chief Accountant and is currently the author of the renowned CCH publication, Revenue Recognition Guide. ARM helps professionals understand and comply with rapidly changing accounting, auditing, governmental, SEC and internal controls standards, including fair value measurements, the FASB Codification, uncertain tax positions, Sarbanes-Oxley and other pertinent topics\\\",\\n2225:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"An Amendment of the FASB Accounting Standards Codification\\\\u00ae \\\\u00b7 No. 2018-09 ... The \\\\u00b7 amendments in this Update include items raised for Board consideration through \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nAccounting Standards Update No. 2016-09, Compensation\\\\u2014Stock Compensation \\\\u00b7 (Topic 718): Improvements to Employee Share-Based Payment Accounting, is \\\\u00b7 unclear on whether an entity should recognize excess tax benefits (or tax \\\\u00b7 deficiencies) for compensation expense that is taken on the entity\\\\u2019s tax return. The \\\\u00b7 amendment to paragraph 718-740-35-2 in this Update clarifies that an entity \\\\u00b7 should recognize excess tax benefits (that is, the difference in tax benefits between \\\\u00b7 the deduction for tax purposes and the compensation cost recognized for financial \\\\u00b7 statement reporting) in the period in which the amount of the deduction is \\\\u00b7 determined. At the November 17-18, 1999 meeting, the Task Force agreed with the EITF \\\\u00b7 Agenda Committee's recommendation that the proposed issue, \\\\\\\"Accounting \\\\u00b7 for Subsequent Investments in an Investee After Suspension of Equity \\\\u00b7 Method Loss Recognition,\\\\\\\" not be added to the EITF agenda at this time. The proposed issue will be reconsidered at a future meeting pending further \\\\u00b7 input from the EITF AcSEC Observer on the status of AcSEC's project on \\\\u00b7 the reconsideration of AICPA Statement of Position 78-9, Accounting for \\\\u00b7 Investments in Real Estate Ventures. Pending EITF or \\\\u00b7 AcSEC resolution of the issues identified in the proposed issue, the SEC \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nStatement on Auditing Standards No. 1 See Topic 855 and AU 560, Subsequent Events (SAS 1 or AU 560), paragraph 3. The first type [of subsequent event] consists of those events that \\\\u00b7 provide additional evidence with respect to conditions that existed \\\\u00b7 at the date of the balance sheet and affect the estimates inherent in \\\\u00b7 the process of preparing financial statements. All information that \\\\u00b7 becomes available prior to the issuance of the financial statements \\\\u00b7 should be used by management in its evaluation of the conditions \\\\u00b7 on which the estimates were based. Announcement: Issue No. 88-16, \\\\\\\"Basis in Leveraged Buyout ...\\\\n\\\\nEquity Method of Accounting for Investments in Common Stock. ... Investee B since 19X2. Through the end of 19X5, Investor A \\\\u00b7 recognized Investee B losses sufficient to reduce its investment to \\\\u00b7 $0. Investor A had no other investments in Investee B, had not \\\\u00b7 guaranteed any obligations of Investee B, and had no obligations or \\\\u00b7 commitments to provide further financial support. As a result, Investor A recognized no additional equity method losses after \\\\u00b7 19X5, although Investee B continued to incur net losses through \\\\u00b7 May 31, 19X9. The additional equity method losses not recognized \\\\u00b7 by Investor A total $25 through May 31, 19X9. If the donor \\\\u00b7 has permanently restricted the NFP\\\\u2019s use of its interest, the contribution \\\\u00b7 shall be classified as an increase in permanently restricted net assets. ... If the NFP has the immediate right to \\\\u00b7 use its interest without restrictions, the contribution shall be classified as ... General) and some jurisdictions follow trust law. 958-205-05-9 Because donor stipulations and laws vary, NFPs must assess the \\\\u00b7\\\",\\n2350:    \\\"text\\\": \\\"OK here's the full picture. It's not a mafia situation \\\\u2014 it's actually the opposite. The door IS open, it's just that the history makes it *look* like there's a secret handshake.\\\\n\\\\n**The timeline that creates the confusion:**\\\\n\\\\nBefore February 2023, the FASB Codification had two tiers: a crippled \\\\\\\"Basic View\\\\\\\" (free, but no search, no copy/paste, limited navigation) and a \\\\\\\"Professional View\\\\\\\" ($940/year per user). PwC, Deloitte, KPMG, Thomson Reuters Checkpoint, Wolters Kluwer CCH \\\\u2014 they all had **licensing agreements** with the Financial Accounting Foundation to reproduce the full codification text within their paid platforms. That's the commercial arrangement: FAF licenses the text, firms pay to embed it in their research tools alongside their own interpretive guides.\\\\n\\\\n**Then the SEC's Investor Advisory Committee told FAF to stop gatekeeping.** In January 2023, FAF announced it was eliminating the paid tier entirely, and in February 2023 they launched full free access at **asc.fasb.org**. The Professional View is gone. Everyone gets everything \\\\u2014 full search, copy/paste, printing, the whole codification, every paragraph of every topic.\\\\n\\\\n**What PwC Viewpoint actually is:**\\\\n\\\\nTwo layers, and the trick is understanding which is which:\\\\n\\\\n1. **Free tier (no account needed):** All PwC guides (the 30+ accounting and reporting guides we identified before), all news, all \\\\\\\"In depth\\\\\\\" publications, AND \\\\u2014 here's the key \\\\u2014 **all US GAAP and US GASB content is free.** That includes the FASB Codification embedded in their platform. Their own pricing flyer says it explicitly: *\\\\\\\"US GAAP and US GASB related content is available free of charge by registering for a free Viewpoint account.\\\\\\\"*\\\\n\\\\n2. **Paid tier ($$$):** PwC's proprietary Accounting and Reporting Manual (ARM), the SEC Volume, IFRS content (which requires a separate license from the IFRS Foundation), and international content. This is PwC's *own* work product \\\\u2014 their internal guidance manuals that their audit teams use.\\\\n\\\\n**The actual access paths you have RIGHT NOW:**\\\\n\\\\n**Path 1: asc.fasb.org (the source)**\\\\n- Full FASB Codification, all ~90 topics, every paragraph\\\\n- Free registration, full search, copy/paste enabled\\\\n- No API, no bulk download \\\\u2014 it's a web application\\\\n- But you CAN manually navigate topic by topic and copy text\\\\n- The terms say \\\\\\\"personal non-commercial use\\\\\\\" unless you get written permission \\\\u2014 but this is the same copyright gray area we discussed (private body writing what functions as law)\\\\n\\\\n**Path 2: Every single ASU (Accounting Standards Update) is a free PDF on fasb.org**\\\\n- These are the *amendments* to the Codification\\\\n- They contain the **full verbatim text** of every paragraph being added/changed/deleted\\\\n- They're at storage.fasb.org \\\\u2014 direct PDF links\\\\n- Example: ASU 2014-09 (Revenue Recognition/ASC 606) contains the entire Topic 606 text\\\\n- You can reconstruct major portions of the current Codification by collecting all the ASUs\\\\n\\\\n**Path 3: PwC Viewpoint free tier**\\\\n- Register free at viewpoint.pwc.com\\\\n- Full ASU text is hosted on their platform (I found verbatim ASC 606 paragraphs there)\\\\n- All their accounting guides contain labeled ASC excerpts with paragraph citations\\\\n- Their guides state this explicitly: *\\\\\\\"excerpts from the FASB's Accounting Standards Codification... clearly labelled\\\\\\\"*\\\\n\\\\n**Path 4: Deloitte DART**\\\\n- The paid tier includes a full Codification browser (their FASB ASC Codification Manual)\\\\n- But the free Roadmap guides contain extensive ASC quotes\\\\n- Every ASU analysis on dart.deloitte.com includes the amendment text\\\\n\\\\n**So what's the actual play for building a GAAP knowledge base?**\\\\n\\\\nYou don't need to scrape anything or find a secret door. The strategy is layered:\\\\n\\\\n1. **Primary source:** Go to asc.fasb.org, register free, and systematically read/copy the topics relevant to your knowledge base. For a tax advisory practice, you probably need maybe 15-20 topics max (revenue recognition, income taxes, leases, financial instruments, etc.)\\\\n\\\\n2. **Supplementary:** Download the key ASU PDFs from storage.fasb.org \\\\u2014 these are unrestricted PDFs on a public server. They contain full paragraph text with tracked changes showing exactly what was added/removed.\\\\n\\\\n3. **Interpretation layer:** PwC's free guides on Viewpoint give you the Big 4's read on how each standard actually works in practice, with direct ASC citations. These are arguably *more* useful than the raw codification because they include the Q&As and examples that practitioners actually need.\\\\n\\\\n4. **The XBRL Taxonomy** (the one I found earlier with the open license) \\\\u2014 this gives you the complete structural map of every element in the Codification. It's machine-readable, explicitly allows reproduction in \\\\\\\"Permitted Works that comment on, explain, or assist in the use or implementation\\\\\\\" \\\\u2014 and includes every paragraph reference.\\\\n\\\\nThe \\\\\\\"mafia\\\\\\\" perception comes from the *old* regime (pre-2023) when FAF was charging $940/year and licensing to the Big 4. The SEC essentially forced them to open it up. The Big 4 still have licensing agreements for embedding the text in their commercial platforms, but you don't need their platforms to get the text \\\\u2014 you can get it from the source for free.\\\",\\n\",\"stderr\":\"\"}"
    }
  },

PwC Viewpoint has TWO tiers:\\n   - FREE tier: All PwC guides, news, AND all US GAAP/GASB content (including FASB Codification)\\n   - PAID tier: PwC's proprietary manuals (ARM - Accounting & Reporting Manual), SEC volume, and IFRS/international content\\n3. PwC's guides literally contain \\\"excerpts from the FASB's Accounting Standards Codification\\\" - clearly labeled, with PwC interpretation alongside\\n\\n\\n4. Let me search for FASB licensing agreements, partnerships, and any backdoors to full codification access.\\\",\\n540:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n559:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n755:    \\\"thinking\\\": \\\"Let me look at this from a different angle. Let me look at what's actually freely available.\\\\n\\\\nAlso, I noticed that PwC Viewpoint has the full ASC text embedded - look at that last search result showing ASC 606 paragraphs verbatim on viewpoint.pwc.com. Let me confirm this actually happened and find out what access looks like now.\\\",\\n1057:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1076:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1285:    \\\"thinking\\\": \\\"The user wants me to dig into how PwC and the other Big 4 firms access the full FASB ASC, and whether there's a way to get similar access. Let me search for this.\\\",\\n1303:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1322:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1519:    \\\"thinking\\\": \\\"Now I'm getting somewhere. Let me piece this together:\\\\n\\\\n1. Since February 2023, FAF eliminated the paid \\\\\\\"Professional View\\\\\\\" and made the FULL FASB ASC available free at asc.fasb.org\\\\n2. PwC Viewpoint has TWO tiers:\\\\n   - FREE tier: All PwC guides, news, AND all US GAAP/GASB content (including FASB Codification)\\\\n   - PAID tier: PwC's proprietary manuals (ARM - Accounting & Reporting Manual), SEC volume, and IFRS/international content\\\\n3. PwC's guides literally contain \\\\\\\"excerpts from the FASB's Accounting Standards Codification\\\\\\\" - clearly labeled, with PwC interpretation alongside\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n4. So there's no secret door here\\\\u2014the entire FASB Codification is already public and free. PwC just has a license to reproduce excerpts in their guides, same as anyone else could. The real story is that FAF actually moved away from the \\\\\\\"mafia\\\\\\\" model by making it free in 2023 after pressure from the SEC's Investor Advisory Committee, whereas they used to charge $940/year for Professional View.\\\\n\\\\nNow I should look into how other commercial databases like RIA Checkpoint fit into this picture.\\\",\\n1545:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1564:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1771:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1790:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1835:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1854:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1978:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n1997:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n2121:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n2140:    \\\"name\\\": \\\"web_search\\\",\\n\",\"stderr\":\"\"}"

    "content": {"returncode":0,"stdout":"The user wants me to dig deeper into how PwC and other Big 4 firms access the full FASB ASC, and whether there's a way to get similar access. Let me search for FASB licensing arrangements, the FAF's relationship with these firms, and any publicly available access paths.\nLet me search more specifically for how PwC and others get licensing to reproduce ASC text, and whether there are alternative paths to access the full codification text.\nLet me try a different angle - look at the actual ASC website terms, and also look at accountinginfo.com which seems to have codification content freely available.\nThe user wants me to dig deeper into how PwC and other Big 4 firms access the full FASB ASC, and whether there's a way to get similar access. Let me search for FASB licensing agreements, partnerships, and any backdoors to full codification access.\nLet me look at this from a different angle. The ASU (Accounting Standards Updates) are freely available as PDFs from FASB. And the ASUs contain the actual codification text changes. Also, the pre-codification standards (FAS statements, etc.) were all published individually. Let me look at what's actually freely available.\\n\\nAlso, I noticed that PwC Viewpoint has the full ASC text embedded - look at that last search result showing ASC 606 paragraphs verbatim on viewpoint.pwc.com.     "text": "OK so here's the raid log \u2014 the actual sequence of doors I tried to open:\n\n**Search 1:** \"FASB Financial Accounting Foundation licensing agreement reproduce codification text\" \u2014 trying to find the contract between FAF and the Big 4. What I found: the licensing agreements exist but aren't public documents. FAF copyrights everything, firms pay to reproduce.\n\n**Search 2:** \"FAF 'reproduced with permission' FASB codification license publishers\" \u2014 looking for the actual permission language. Found university library guides that confirmed: Thomson Reuters Checkpoint, Wolters Kluwer CCH, and the Big 4 all have commercial licenses. But no public terms.\n\n**Search 3:** \"accountinginfo.com FASB ASC codification full text topics free\" \u2014 I'd noticed a site that appeared to have ASC text freely available. Checked if someone had just... put it up. Found: Deloitte DART has a codification browser, Baruch College library guides reference it, but the actual text is behind logins.\n\n**The pivot moment (thinking block):** *\"Let me look at this from a different angle. The ASUs are freely available as PDFs from FASB. And the ASUs contain the actual codification text changes. Also, I noticed that PwC Viewpoint has the full ASC text embedded \u2014 look at that search result showing ASC 606 paragraphs verbatim on viewpoint.pwc.com. Let me dig into this.\"*\n\n**Web fetch \u2014 probing PwC Viewpoint directly:** I fetched `viewpoint.pwc.com/dt/us/en/fasb_financial_accou/asus_fulltext/2014/asu_201409revenue_fr/...` \u2014 an actual URL path that had verbatim FASB Codification amendments to ASC 606 sitting right there. The page loaded. The text was there. That's when I knew PwC was hosting actual codification text on their free tier.\n\n**Search 4:** \"FAF FASB codification free professional view eliminated 2023 full access everyone\" \u2014 this was the breakthrough search. Found the announcement that in January 2023, FAF eliminated the $940/year paywall entirely.\n\n**Search 5:** \"PwC Viewpoint subscription full FASB codification text licensed content pricing\" \u2014 found PwC's own pricing flyer that explicitly says: *\"US GAAP and US GASB related content is available free of charge by registering for a free Viewpoint account.\"*\n\n**Search 6:** \"asc.fasb.org terms conditions copy paste scraping bulk download permitted\" \u2014 checking what the actual rules are. Found: \"personal non-commercial use\" permitted, copyright claimed by FAF, but the same legal gray area (private org writing mandatory law).\n\n**Searches 7-10:** Thomson Reuters Checkpoint pricing, Wolters Kluwer CCH access, XBRL taxonomy licensing, FASB storage.fasb.org ASU PDFs \u2014 mapping every alternative door.\n\nSo the investigation wasn't \"look in the code\" in a developer-tools sense \u2014 it was systematically trying every door on the building. Front entrance (asc.fasb.org), side entrance (storage.fasb.org ASU PDFs), the Big 4's private lobbies (PwC Viewpoint, Deloitte DART), the commercial platforms (Checkpoint, CCH), and the machine-readable basement (XBRL taxonomy). \n\nAnd your instinct was right the whole time: for 14 years, the \"front door\" was deliberately crippled (no search, no copy/paste in the free Basic View), while the Big 4 had full reproduction rights through licensing deals. The SEC had to literally tell FAF to stop it. Your QB backside rush instinct \u2014 \"something about this doesn't add up\" \u2014 was detecting a real thing that existed from 2009 to 2023.",
